Required Findings: Project should follow modular and scalable architecture
Actual Findings: Entire project was initially implemented in a single file, causing poor readability and scalability issues
Resolution: Codebase refactored and split into Models, Services, UI, Storage, Data, and Utils layers

Required Findings: Application data should be persistent and scalable
Actual Findings: Data was stored directly inside the code, resulting in loss of data on restart and no persistence
Resolution: In code data storage was discarded and external file‑based storage approach evaluated

Required Findings: Storage format should be easy to manage, readable, and efficient
Actual Findings: JSON implementation worked but required complex parsing logic and increased code complexity
Resolution: JSON approach dropped due to maintainability concerns

Required Findings: Data format should be lightweight and structured
Actual Findings: CSV storage reduced complexity but required additional logic to format and display data in console
Resolution: CSV approach accepted temporarily but further simplification explored

Deliverable Reviewed: TXT File Storage with Custom Separator
Required Findings: Storage should be simple, readable, and easy to parse
Actual Findings: TXT file with �|� separator proved easy to parse, readable, and efficient for console output
Resolution: TXT based storage finalized for employee data persistence

Required Findings: Data should be printed in a user friendly format
Actual Findings: Repeated print logic caused code duplication and increased file size
Resolution: Common print logic extracted into utility methods

Required Findings: Project should avoid redundant code and follow DRY principles
Actual Findings: Repeated code for input scanning, ID generation, and date handling found
Resolution: Utilities created for print, scan, ID generator, and date utilities
